executiveSummary: Scan of https://www.example-retailer.com analysed 5 page(s) across 8 inclusivity dimensions. Overall inclusivity score: 47/100. 2 dimension(s) comply; 6 require attention. Key concerns: Gender, Email Internationalization (EAI), Civil / Marital Status, Age, Race & Ethnicity, Legal Document.

issues: No inclusive/neutral gender option ("Non-binary", "Other", "Prefer not to say") found.
recommendations: Add a "Prefer not to say" and/or "Non-binary / Other" option to all gender selects.
Replace Mr./Mrs./Ms. with an optional Mx. honorific.
Use gender-neutral copy ("your order", "the account holder") throughout.
Allow users to skip gender fields entirely if not business-critical.

salesImpact.currentImpact: Binary gender fields alienate non-binary and transgender customers, causing form abandonment at registration/checkout. Studies show 1–4% of population identifies outside binary gender.
salesImpact.futureImpact: Gen Z (>25% of purchasing power by 2030) expects inclusive UX as baseline. Brands perceived as exclusionary lose share to competitors.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Inclusive gender fields increase registration completion rates by ~3–7%, reduce support contacts, and boost NPS among LGBTQ+ segments.

findings.issueSummary: Unicode email (e.g., josé@correo.es) was rejected by the form validator.
issues: Unicode email (e.g., josé@correo.es) was rejected by the form validator.
Rejection message observed: "Por favor, introduce una dirección de correo válida."
recommendations: Validate email addresses against RFC 5322 extended with RFC 6532 (Unicode local-part support).
Remove browser-based type="email" HTML5 validation in favour of server-side EAI-aware validation.
Test with: josé@correo.es, 用户@例子.广告, Ñoño@empresa.com.mx.

salesImpact.currentImpact: Rejecting Unicode emails (RFC 6532) blocks customers with non-ASCII names — a large segment in LATAM, Asia and among diaspora communities. Direct revenue loss per rejected registration.
salesImpact.futureImpact: As global ecommerce grows, EAI adoption will become a compliance expectation. Delayed migration increases technical debt.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Accepting EAI emails removes a silent conversion blocker, estimates suggest 0.5–2% registration uplift in international markets.

findings.issueSummary: Nationality field not requested in scanned flows.
recommendations: Replace closed nationality selects with a searchable free-text field with auto-complete.
If a closed list is required, add "Other / Prefer to self-describe" with a text input.
Evaluate whether nationality data is legally required; if not, remove the field.

salesImpact.currentImpact: Closed nationality lists with no self-description option exclude customers who do not identify with listed nationalities, increasing dropout and complaints.
salesImpact.futureImpact: Cross-border commerce regulation (e.g., EU Digital Markets Act) may require inclusive identity fields.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Open or self-description nationality fields reduce friction for international customers and improve data quality.

findings.issueSummary: Country selector has limited coverage (28 options). Many countries likely missing.
issues: Country selector has limited coverage (28 options). Many countries likely missing.
recommendations: Use a comprehensive ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country list (195+ countries).
Implement a searchable/auto-complete country selector.
Do not pre-select or hard-code a single country.
Provide localised country names where possible.

salesImpact.currentImpact: Country selectors missing key emerging markets exclude potential customers before the purchase is even attempted.
salesImpact.futureImpact: APAC and Africa represent the fastest-growing ecommerce markets. Limited country coverage directly caps addressable market.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Comprehensive country coverage enables cross-border sales, reduces customer support overhead, and signals global ambition.

findings.issueSummary: Civil status options do not include non-heteronormative choices (domestic partnership, civil union).
issues: Civil status options do not include non-heteronormative choices (domestic partnership, civil union).
recommendations: Add: "Civil union / domestic partnership", "Cohabiting", "Prefer not to say" options.
Evaluate whether civil status is legally required; if not, remove the field.
Use gender-neutral labels: "Partner" instead of "Husband/Wife".

salesImpact.currentImpact: Requiring civil status without inclusive options excludes same-sex couples and non-traditional family structures.
salesImpact.futureImpact: Legal frameworks in numerous markets now recognise diverse family structures; non-compliance creates regulatory exposure.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Inclusive civil status options improve completeness of customer data and signal brand inclusivity.

findings.issueSummary: Date of birth is marked as required in one or more forms.
issues: Date of birth is marked as required in one or more forms.
Age-stereotyped language found: "para jóvenes" on https://www.example-retailer.com/
recommendations: Only collect date of birth where legally required (e.g., age-restricted products).
Replace DOB input with a simple age-confirmation checkbox for age-gating.
Audit UX copy for age-segmented language; replace with inclusive alternatives.
Apply data minimisation principle per GDPR Art. 5(1)(c).

salesImpact.currentImpact: Mandatory date-of-birth fields deter privacy-conscious customers. Age-stereotyped copy alienates both younger and older segments.
salesImpact.futureImpact: GDPR and CCPA impose data minimisation; collecting unnecessary DOB data increases compliance risk.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Removing unnecessary DOB requirements reduces registration friction. Age-neutral UX copy broadens appeal across all demographics.

findings.issueSummary: Visual diversity appears moderate; room for improvement on hero/landing pages (approximate heuristic). Approximate heuristic based on image count and alt-text variety. Does NOT identify individuals.
issues: Visual diversity appears moderate; room for improvement on hero/landing pages (approximate heuristic).
recommendations: Audit hero and landing page imagery; ensure representation across diverse contexts and skin tones.
Include imagery from varied cultural contexts, not just symbolic/tokenistic representation.
Consult with diverse focus groups when selecting campaign imagery.
goodPracticeExamples: Fenty Beauty (inclusive imagery across all pages)

salesImpact.currentImpact: Limited visual representation on hero pages signals exclusion to minority ethnic customers, correlating with lower engagement.
salesImpact.futureImpact: Diverse representation in brand imagery is increasingly a purchasing criterion, especially for Gen Z and millennial consumers.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Diverse imagery improves brand perception, reduces bounce rates, and expands emotional resonance across customer segments.

findings.issueSummary: Only national ID (DNI) accepted — foreign residents and international customers excluded.
issues: Only national ID (DNI) accepted — foreign residents and international customers excluded.
Passport not listed as an accepted document type.
No foreign resident document (NIE or equivalent) accepted.
recommendations: Accept at minimum: national ID (DNI), foreign resident ID (NIE), Passport, and EU Driving Licence.
For international flows, accept: Passport, national ID from any country, residence permit.
Validate document numbers without country-specific hard-coding.
goodPracticeExamples: N26 (passport + national ID + foreign resident ID)

salesImpact.currentImpact: Accepting only national ID (DNI) excludes foreign residents, tourists, and recent immigrants — real purchasing power often in premium segments.
salesImpact.futureImpact: Regulatory pressure to accept multiple ID types (e.g., EU eIDAS 2.0) is increasing. Early compliance reduces future migration cost.
salesImpact.benefitIfResolved: Multi-document acceptance removes a hard blocker for foreign residents and cross-border customers, directly unlocking revenue.

evidence.description: Form page: Registration – gender + civil status fields
Form page: Checkout – country + document type fields
Landing page hero imagery (visual diversity assessment)
